subject: STAP replication, rescuing biomed research and other stories from F1000
body:
To ensure you receive emails from Faculty of 1000, add info@updates.f1000.com to your safelist.

-----------------------------------------------------

Faculty of 1000 |  http://f1000.com                
Monthly Update  |  June 2014   

-----------------------------------------------------

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
 STAP replication paper published in F1000Research:    
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 
In a study published in F1000Research [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-102/v1 ] in May, Professor Kenneth Lee of the Chinese University of Hong Kong reveals the full experimental results of an attempt to replicate a controversial study [ http://f1000.com/prime/718255373?key=GkUyujpPPK6aSwE ] that suggested that bathing somatic cells in acid can reprogram them to induced pluripotent stem (iPS) cells. With systematically collected and fully available data, Lee and his colleagues report that carefully replicating the original acid treatment method does not induce pluripotency in two types of mouse somatic cells, including those used in the original study. Read more on our blog [ http://blog.f1000research.com/2014/05/08/open-study-hopes-to-put-stap-in-the-past/ ].   
 

   F1000Prime                                
==================

994 recommendations [ http://f1000pri.me/past30days ] added by our Faculty of 10,000+ Members in the past 30 days*. Below is a selection:

16 *   
Rescuing research: a call to action   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718348298?key=tKppqrC3Wao8lUO ]   
This gripping policy paper is written by accomplished scientists who are decrying the shortcomings of the US research enterprise.
Alberts B, Kirschner M, Tilghman S et al. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2014; 111:5773-7

6 *    
It takes two    
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718338044?key=OxIucLDQ2PtZWrF ]   
A gradient of just two proteins is enough to turn a zebrafish blastula into a fully developed embryo with head, trunk and tail.
Xu P, Houssin N, Ferri-Lagneau K et al. Science. 2014; 344:87-9

12 *   
Get with the reprogram    
[ http://f1000.com/prime/718363634?key=0JrBz9yIxpCchF8 ]
A set of transcription factors was sufficient to reprogram committed murine blood cells to induced hematopoietic stem cells, providing new strategies for regenerative medicine.
Riddell J, Gazit R, Garrison B et al. Cell. 2014; 157:549-64

*Accurate at the time of writing.


 F1000Prime Reports
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ 

Open-access review journal [ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/ ], reporting on the hottest topics.


Developments in HIV-1 immunotherapy and therapeutic vaccination   
[ http://f1000.com/prime/reports/m/6/43 ]   
Recent advances in immunotherapy and therapeutic vaccination have the potential to better control HIV-1 infection, allow structured treatment holidays to minimize side-effects and even establish a functional cure. 
F1000Prime Report by Peter Lawrence Smith, Helen Tanner, and Angus Dalgleish [ http://f1000.com/prime/thefaculty/member/825902941303033 ].   
F1000Prime Rep 2014, 6:43   


 ---------------
 See all recent F1000Prime Reports          
 >> http://f1000.com/prime/reports/                 
 ---------------   
    

----------------------

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 F1000 Specialists programme    
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 

Become an F1000 Specialist. Click to find out more. [ http://f1000.com/specialists ]

---------------------- 



   F1000Research                      
======================

Our innovative open access journal [ http://f1000research.com/ ] offering rapid publication, open peer review and open data.   

Below is a selection of our latest peer reviewed articles:    

RESEARCH ARTICLE   
Transient acid treatment cannot induce neonatal somatic cells to become pluripotent stem cells [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-102/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3dq ]   
Latest Published: 08 May 2014    
Tang MK, Lo LM, Shi WT et al.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:102   

DATA ARTICLE   
Neurosharing: large-scale data sets (spike, LFP) recorded from the hippocampal-entorhinal system in behaving rats [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-98/v1 ] [v2; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/37v ]   
Latest Published: 30 Apr 2014   
Mizuseki K, Diba K, Pastalkova E et al.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:98   

OPINION ARTICLE
Computational science: shifting the focus from tools to models [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-101/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/3af  ]   
Published: 07 May 2014   
Hinsen K   
F1000Research 2014, 3:101   

WEB TOOL   
Recommendations to enable drug development for inherited neuropathies: Charcot-Marie-Tooth and Giant Axonal Neuropathy [ http://f1000research.com/articles/3-95/v1 ] [v1; ref status: indexed, http://f1000r.es/38s ]   
Latest Published: 24 Apr 2014   
Dai Z, Sheridan JM, Gearing LJ et al.   
F1000Research 2014, 3:95   

~~~~~~~~~
 This summer, submit a Data Note to F1000Research   
~~~~~~~~~

A Data Note is a formal publication with data and methods, but no analysis or conclusion. It ensures that your data remain discoverable, accessible and usable. Until July 31 2014, submission of your first Data Note is free for datasets of up to 1GB. Find out more [ http://f1000research.com/data-notes ].



 ---------------------------------------------
  Submit now to F1000Research          
  >> http://f1000research.com/author/submit        
 ----------------------------------------------
 
   
 
    F1000Posters                 
======================
 
Open Repository [ http://f1000.com/posters ] for posters and slide presentations; completely free to view and submit. Below are a few selections:


* CHEMICAL BIOLOGY *  

Investigation of the role of ROS modulation in the antitumour effect of a novel breast cancer drug. 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1095652 ]   
Martinez-Perez C, Mullen P, Ward C et al.   
F1000Posters 2014, 5:385  

* ECOLOGY *

Odour signals sex, rank and genotype in an Old World monkey. 
[ http://f1000.com/posters/browse/summary/1095629 ]   
Setchell J, Vaglio S, Abbott K et al.   
F1000Posters 2014, 5:403   


 ----------------------------------------------
  Deposit now        
  >> http://f1000.com/posters/fordepositors      
 ---------------------------------------------- 

  

  
----------------------

 * F1000Tips *

Follow @F1000Tips on Twitter [ http://twitter.com/f1000tips ] for tips, tricks, and customer support for all Faculty of 1000 service.

---------------------- 


-----------------------------------------------------   

In addition to this Monthly Update, we also send out emails featuring deals and discounts for registered users. To sign up, registered users should visit MyF1000 via any F1000 product and opt-in to receive "Information from F1000 about special offers" (under Email Alerts).

You have received this message because you asked to receive the F1000 Monthly Update to your registered email address from Faculty of 1000. If you do not wish to receive any further such messages, you may unsubscribe [ http://www.f1000.com/unsubscribe/email?email=jeeproject@yahoo.com&emailAlertType=MONTHLY_UPDATES ] at any time. For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at info@f1000.com [ mailto:info@f1000.com ].

Middlesex House, 34-42 Cleveland Street | London, W1T 4LB | United Kingdom
